rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical halogen-shuttle (e-shuttle) reaction that transfers Br2 or Cl2 from vicinal dihaloalkane donors (e.g., 1,2-dibromoethane, polychloroalkanes including Lindane) to alkene acceptors to give 1,2-dihalogenated products with extrusion of ethylene.
electrochemical_role: Paired electrolysis: cathodic 2e- reduction of the vicinal dihaloalkane donor cleaves two C-X bonds releasing 2 X- and ethylene; anodic 2e- oxidation regenerates X+ (as bromonium or, for Cl2, as Mn(III)-Cl from a Mn(II) mediator) which halogenates the alkene acceptor.

key_intermediates.name: bromonium ion / Br+ equivalent on alkene

electron_flow: Cathode delivers 2 e- to the vicinal dihaloalkane donor, cleaving two C-X bonds to release 2 X- and ethylene; the X- is oxidized at the anode (directly for Br-, or via a Mn(II)/Mn(III) mediator for Cl-) to an X+ equivalent that adds across the alkene acceptor, with a second X- closing the addition to give the 1,2-dihalide product.
rate_determining_step: Second cathodic electron transfer to the dihaloalkane donor (irreversible CV wave at ca. -2.7 V vs Fc, coupled to extrusion of ethylene + bromide)

probe_reagents.name: 1,2-dibromoethane (donor probe)
probe_reagents.observation: Acts as Br2 donor; no HBr elimination products observed, supporting C-Br homolytic/2e- cleavage with ethylene extrusion rather than E2.

probe_reagents.name: 2,3-dibromobutane (donor probe)
probe_reagents.observation: Delivers Br2 to alkene acceptor; absence of HX elimination products confirms shuttle (not elimination) pathway.

probe_reagents.name: 1,1,1,2-tetrachloroethane
probe_reagents.observation: Functions as Cl2 donor without HX elimination.

probe_reagents.name: 1,2-dichloroethane (DCE)
probe_reagents.observation: Cl2 donor in Mn-mediated e-shuttle; absence of HCl elimination supports proposed mechanism.

probe_reagents.name: Lindane (γ-HCH)
probe_reagents.observation: Serves as Cl2 donor; benzene confirmed as co-product by crude NMR and GC, consistent with sequential dechlorination/extrusion.

notes: Br2 shuttle proceeds without a transition-metal mediator (direct anodic oxidation of Br- to Br+/bromonium). Cl2 shuttle requires a Mn(II)/Mn(III) redox mediator because direct anodic oxidation of Cl- is too high in potential. The irreversibility of the second cathodic reduction is attributed to fast chemical follow-up (loss of ethylene + bromide), and N2 sparging removes ethylene to prevent reverse reaction. A representative intramolecular Br2 transfer experiment supports the proposed two-electron shuttle picture; HX elimination pathway was explicitly ruled out across multiple donors.
